ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -s option Discard the first count lines read.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Discard the first count lines read.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -t option Remove a trailing delim (default newline) from each line read.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Remove a trailing delim (default newline) from each line read.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -u option Read lines from file descriptor fd instead of the standard input.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Read lines from file descriptor fd instead of the standard input.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -C option Evaluate callback each time quantum lines are read.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Evaluate callback each time quantum lines are read.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -c option Specify the number of lines read between each call to callback .
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Specify the number of lines read between each call to callback .

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -a option The words are assigned to sequential indices of the array variable aname , starting at 0.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The words are assigned to sequential indices of the array variable aname , starting at 0.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -d option The first character of delim terminates the input line, rather than newline.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The first character of delim terminates the input line, rather than newline.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -e option If the standard input is coming from a terminal, read uses Readline (see Command Line Editing ) to obtain the line.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If the standard input is coming from a terminal, read uses Readline (see Command Line Editing ) to obtain the line.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -i option If Readline is being used to read the line, read places text into the editing buffer before editing begins.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If Readline is being used to read the line, read places text into the editing buffer before editing begins.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -n option read returns after reading nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out, but honors a delimiter if it reads fewer than nchars characters before the delimiter.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: read returns after reading nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out, but honors a delimiter if it reads fewer than nchars characters before the delimiter.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -N option read returns after reading exactly nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: read returns after reading exactly nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -p option Display prompt , without a trailing newline, before attempting to read any input, but only if input is coming from a terminal.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Display prompt , without a trailing newline, before attempting to read any input, but only if input is coming from a terminal.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -r option If this option is given, backslash does not act as an escape character.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If this option is given, backslash does not act as an escape character.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -s option If input is coming from a terminal, characters are not echoed.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If input is coming from a terminal, characters are not echoed.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -t option Cause read to time out and return failure if it does not read a complete line of input (or a specified number of characters) within timeout seconds.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Cause read to time out and return failure if it does not read a complete line of input (or a specified number of characters) within timeout seconds.
